jokes.text: Who likes good news? Everyone? Then, good news, everyone. Our next delivery isn't to some dangerous outer space planet.
jokes.setup: Professor's typical 'good news everyone' opener suggesting something positive
jokes.punchline: The misdirection that NOT going to outer space is the good news
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor misdirection - the 'good news' is avoiding danger, which subverts expectations. Well-structured with his signature delivery.

jokes.text: Earth is dangerous. I fell off my chair there once.
jokes.setup: Professor saying they're going to Earth instead of dangerous outer space
jokes.punchline: Fry's ridiculously mundane example of Earth being dangerous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - taking the most innocuous thing and treating it as genuinely dangerous. Great character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: Quit it, Earth.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about falling off his chair on Earth
jokes.punchline: Addressing Earth like it's a misbehaving person
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical absurdist line. Fry's childlike way of anthropomorphizing the planet.

jokes.text: Long Long Island
jokes.setup: Futuristic setting requiring updated place names
jokes.punchline: Extension of Long Island to 'Long Long Island'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay extension gag. Functional worldbuilding humor but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It looks like a pair of clamps.
jokes.setup: Bender's dramatic buildup about using special robot vision
jokes.punchline: Completely obvious visual observation delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimatic payoff to Bender's buildup. The obviousness is the joke.

jokes.text: These are all rusted out with snitch juice.
jokes.setup: Clamps complaining about his old clamps being rusted
jokes.punchline: Implication that the rust is from blood of informants
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny euphemism that perfectly establishes Clamps' violent character. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Oh, geez, I forgot my Pope manners.
jokes.setup: Donbot warning Clamps about not clamping in front of the Space Pope
jokes.punchline: Violent gangster worried about etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - the most violent character concerned about manners. Nice juxtaposition.

jokes.text: Dearly beloved, it is with great speed that I now pronounce you husband and wife.
jokes.setup: Traditional wedding ceremony opening
jokes.punchline: Space Pope rushing through ceremony 'with great speed'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay on 'great speed' instead of traditional wedding language. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: May I help you with some assistance?
jokes.setup: Formal greeting at the door
jokes.punchline: Redundant phrasing typical of Donbot's speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Classic Donbot verbal tic - redundant phrasing is part of his character voice.

jokes.text: We don't got nobody here by that alias. Hey, hey, that's me.
jokes.setup: Donbot denying anyone by that name is there
jokes.punchline: Clamps immediately contradicting him and claiming the package
jokes.explanation: Good setup-payoff with Clamps undermining the Donbot's attempt at secrecy. Nice character interaction.

jokes.text: Your name's Francis?
jokes.setup: Clamps' tough guy persona
jokes.punchline: Revelation of his surprisingly gentle real name
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - the violent 'Clamps' having a gentle name like Francis. Sets up running gag.

jokes.text: You forget, Leela. I'm Bender, owner of disguise.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming he can successfully disguise himself
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous phrasing 'owner of disguise'
jokes.explanation: Bender's characteristic overconfident boasting with awkward phrasing. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Piscopo. This guy.
jokes.setup: Calculon performing at a mob wedding
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reference to 80s comedian Joe Piscopo
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference with minimal context or twist. Economical but relies on recognition rather than craft.

jokes.text: I'm the bride. Oops.
jokes.setup: Bender hitting on someone at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Awkward revelation he's hitting on the bride
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward situation comedy. Bender's 'oops' is perfectly understated for the situation.

jokes.text: Bender, it's me, Fanny. You who? The Donbot's wife? We had an affair?
jokes.setup: Woman recognizing Bender at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Bender completely forgetting their affair with the mob boss's wife
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's promiscuity and terrible memory creating dangerous situations.

jokes.text: I don't want to hit on anybody I already had sex with.
jokes.setup: Bender realizing he needs to be more careful at the wedding
jokes.punchline: His concern is avoiding repeat encounters, not infidelity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - his concern isn't morality but efficiency. Great character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: You're my third choice.
jokes.setup: Bender approaching another woman at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Bluntly honest pickup line about her ranking
jokes.explanation: Hilariously blunt and terrible pickup line. Perfect economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, Bender, this is all happening too slowly. Let's get married. Quiet, I'm making out with a floozy.
jokes.setup: Woman wanting to escalate relationship quickly
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissing her while busy with someone else
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with Bender's typical callousness. His blunt rejection is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Hold your clapper.
jokes.setup: Need to be quiet
jokes.punchline: Robot-specific variation on 'hold your tongue'
jokes.explanation: Nice robot-specific phrase variation. Good worldbuilding humor with decent originality.

jokes.text: It gives me no joy to do this, because Clamps will be doing it.
jokes.setup: Donbot apologizing for what he's about to do
jokes.punchline: The reason for no joy is that he won't get to do the violence himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor subversion - the regret isn't about the violence but about missing out on it. Great character work.

jokes.text: No, no. Line? No...!
jokes.setup: Calculon about to be murdered
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to ask for his line mid-death scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - Calculon so committed to dramatic performance he needs direction even during his own murder. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: And guys who mess around with his daughters. And attempt to duplicate his meatball recipe.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of things her father hates
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous inclusion of recipe duplication alongside serious offenses
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - treating recipe theft as seriously as adultery. Nice character detail for mob boss.

jokes.text: Says who? I didn't witness anything!
jokes.setup: Amy asking how the wedding was
jokes.punchline: Bender's panicked denial before being accused of anything
jokes.explanation: Classic guilty conscience reaction - denying things he wasn't accused of. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: All right, Amy, you win! The mob did it. I saw the whole thing while also having hot sex with the Donbot's daughter.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to keep quiet about witnessing the crime
jokes.punchline: Immediately confessing everything including irrelevant sexual details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender's inability to keep secrets and tendency to overshare inappropriate details.

jokes.text: I'm scared and great at sex.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing his fear about the mob
jokes.punchline: Adding completely irrelevant boast about sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - even while scared, he can't resist bragging. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: And then I ate some excellent meatballs.
jokes.setup: Bender recounting the dramatic murder scene
jokes.punchline: Ending with irrelevant food review
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier meatball reference, shows Bender's misplaced priorities during traumatic events.

jokes.text: No, no. Can I have another $50? No. No? No! No...!
jokes.setup: FBI agent rejecting Bender's request for more money
jokes.punchline: Bender mimicking Calculon's death scene for comedic effect
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback execution - Bender dramatically recreating the death scene in a completely inappropriate context.

jokes.text: All rise for the Honorable Judge 724. Good day. I'd like to say I'm prepared to tolerate several if not all forms of intimidation during this trial.
jokes.setup: Second judge replacing the intimidated first judge
jokes.punchline: Openly admitting to being corrupt and easily intimidated
jokes.explanation: Good corruption humor - judge openly admitting to being compromised. Nice escalation from the previous judge.

jokes.text: Your Honor, the prosecution pecks for a mistrial on the grounds of jury tampering.
jokes.setup: Legal proceeding with prosecutor making objection
jokes.punchline: Bird-like prosecutor using 'pecks' instead of 'asks'
jokes.explanation: Simple species-based wordplay. Functional character humor but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: People call our star witness Mr. X, whose appearance will be altered be-caw-se he's in grave danger.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor explaining witness protection measures
jokes.punchline: Bird prosecutor's 'caw' sound interrupting normal speech
jokes.explanation: Continuation of bird prosecutor gag. Simple sound-based humor, somewhat repetitive.

jokes.text: Aah, fix it, fix it, fix it, fix it!
jokes.setup: Bender's disguise being compromised
jokes.punchline: Panicked reaction trying to fix his appearance
jokes.explanation: Basic panic reaction humor. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: including a crime you made up yourself called 'burgle-arson-arceny'? That's a wholly owned trademark of Rodriguez Crime Concepts, Inc.
jokes.setup: Defense attorney accusing Bender of making up crimes
jokes.punchline: Bender proudly defending his trademarked crime invention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - trademarking made-up crimes. Perfect Bender entrepreneurship and the portmanteau crime name is genius.

jokes.text: You are charged with two counts of burgle-arson-arceny. How do you plead? Not inno-guilty-cent.
jokes.setup: Bender being charged with his own invented crime
jokes.punchline: His confused plea combining innocent, guilty, and a cent
jokes.explanation: Good callback execution with Bender's characteristically confused legal plea. Nice wordplay structure.

jokes.text: Wait, I'm not finished. ...o! Now I'm finished.
jokes.setup: Judge trying to stop Bender's testimony
jokes.punchline: Bender insisting on completing his Calculon impression
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Bender's obsessive need to complete his impression despite legal proceedings.

jokes.text: Your Honor, the prosecution roosts.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor ending their case presentation
jokes.punchline: Using 'roosts' instead of 'rests' due to bird nature
jokes.explanation: Another species-based pun from the bird prosecutor. Predictable but maintains character consistency.

jokes.text: After a steamy 12-way deliberation, we find the accused guilty as... Wait. The Donbot is innocent.
jokes.setup: Jury announcing their verdict after deliberation
jokes.punchline: Sudden reversal mid-sentence due to obvious intimidation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing obvious jury intimidation. The 'steamy 12-way' adds absurdist element.

jokes.text: My personal trainer, and he's trained me to say that my wounds were self-inflicted, for you see I am a big Hollywood idiot dumb guy what does dat sort of ting.
jokes.setup: Calculon appearing alive and explaining his survival
jokes.punchline: Transparently coached statement with terrible fake accent
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor with Calculon's obviously coached and terrible performance. The accent adds to the artificiality.

jokes.text: Poifect.
jokes.setup: Calculon's terrible coached performance
jokes.punchline: Donbot's satisfied 'perfect' pronunciation as 'poifect'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice - Donbot's accent and satisfaction with the obviously terrible performance.

jokes.text: Honey, if you're marrying Bender, you might want to get a black dress for the wedding. That's going to look hot.
jokes.setup: Woman suggesting black dress for wedding
jokes.punchline: Bender missing the death threat implication and focusing on attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Bender completely missing the funeral dress implication. Typical character obliviousness.

jokes.text: You'll be given a new identity, a new place to live, and this iPod. I don't want it anymore.
jokes.setup: FBI agent listing important witness protection benefits
jokes.punchline: Including his unwanted personal iPod as if it's an official benefit
jokes.explanation: Nice anticlimax humor mixing official benefits with personal throwaway item. Good economy.

jokes.text: Before I leave, I just want you to know I've never done any work whatsoever.
jokes.setup: Bender saying goodbye to the Professor
jokes.punchline: His final honest confession about never working
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his deathbed confession is about his laziness, not emotional connection. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Zoidberg, I know it seems like I can't stand you.
jokes.setup: Bender appearing to start heartfelt goodbye to Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: The implication that he stops there, confirming he can't stand him
jokes.explanation: Great character beat - Bender starting what seems like a heartfelt moment then abandoning it. The silence is the punchline.

jokes.text: Me, too. I'll need a butler.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing wish to go with Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's agreement is purely self-serving about needing a butler
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - seeming emotional agreement that's actually selfish. Perfect Bender character work.

jokes.text: There'll never be another like him. Let's hire someone better.
jokes.setup: Professor appearing to mourn Bender's uniqueness
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting sentiment by wanting to hire someone better
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. Professor's callous practicality subverts the sentimental setup beautifully.

jokes.text: Yeah, and then I'll introduce him to my best friend-- clamp number one!
jokes.setup: Donbot instructing Clamps to befriend Fry
jokes.punchline: Clamps' idea of friendship introduction is violence with his clamp
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Clamps can't think of any interaction that doesn't involve violence. Nice personification of his clamp.

jokes.text: Francis... Francis? I'll clamp you with the Francis!
jokes.setup: Donbot calling Clamps by his real name to calm him down
jokes.punchline: Clamps threatening to use his own name as a weapon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist callback - Clamps treating his own gentle name as a threat. Perfect character absurdity.

jokes.text: Yeah, they all kind of broke.
jokes.setup: Professor ending interviews with other job candidates
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he broke all the previous candidates
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan dark humor. Professor's casual attitude toward destroying job candidates is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Snipping? Looks like you may be out of a job, Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Francis demonstrating he can also do snipping
jokes.punchline: Professor immediately threatening Zoidberg's job security
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's callousness toward Zoidberg. Typical workplace threat humor.

jokes.text: Stay off my turf!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg being threatened by Francis taking over his snipping duties
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg showing uncharacteristic aggression defending his job
jokes.explanation: Good character development - usually meek Zoidberg showing fight when his one skill is threatened.

jokes.text: That guy can bite my shiny metal clamps.
jokes.setup: Francis being insulted by Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Variation on Bender's famous catchphrase using 'clamps' instead of 'ass'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback variation - Clamps adapting Bender's famous line to his own anatomy. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Eh, four stinking years of clamping school for this?!
jokes.setup: Francis having to do mundane delivery work
jokes.punchline: Revelation that there's an actual clamping school with a four-year program
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist worldbuilding - the idea of a formal education for criminal clamping. Great character backstory humor.

jokes.text: Squeezers? Squeezers?! They're clamps, you (bleep)!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg complimenting Francis's 'squeezers'
jokes.punchline: Francis's violent reaction to the wrong terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Clamps's professional pride in correct terminology. Nice escalation from compliment to rage.

jokes.text: Hot diggity! You fellers want to stick around for the hanging tomorrow? It's gonna be a doozy. I sure do love hanging people.
jokes.setup: Sheriff receiving rope delivery
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic excitement about hanging people with old-fashioned enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with cheerful attitude toward execution. The old-west character voice works well.

jokes.text: I like how you didn't get drunk and steal anyone's organs, like our old robot.
jokes.setup: Sheriff praising Francis's work performance
jokes.punchline: Revealing Bender's past behavior as the baseline for comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how terrible Bender was at the job. Nice character consistency for Bender's criminal nature.

jokes.text: We're in space, where no one can hear you clamp.
jokes.setup: Francis explaining why he can kill Bender quietly
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Alien' tagline 'in space no one can hear you scream'
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody callback with perfect character integration. Clamps adapting famous horror line to his method.

jokes.text: I checked on a guy once. I checked him into tiny pieces!
jokes.setup: Francis agreeing to check on Bender with Fry
jokes.punchline: Dark pun on 'checked' meaning violently dismembered
jokes.explanation: Great dark wordplay - 'checked on' vs 'checked into pieces'. Perfect Clamps character voice and economy.

jokes.text: Billy West? What a stupid, phony, made-up name.
jokes.setup: Bender introducing himself as Billy West
jokes.punchline: Fry criticizing the name, not knowing it's the voice actor's real name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Fry insulting the actual voice actor's name. Great inside joke for fans with perfect ironic delivery.

jokes.text: Well, my boyfriend and I were going to have sex on a burning flag, but I could have a bite.
jokes.setup: Billy/Bender mentioning city folk activities of premarital sex and flag-burning
jokes.punchline: Leela combining both activities into one absurd act
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and combination of the two 'sinful' activities Billy mentioned. Leela playing along with the stereotype.

jokes.text: Folks say my mama was a hoe.
jokes.setup: Billy explaining his farming background
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'hoe' - garden tool vs. derogatory term
jokes.explanation: Classic farming pun with double meaning. Works well for the hillbilly character persona.

jokes.text: Which is weird, seeing as how my daddy was a forklift.
jokes.setup: Billy claiming he can't lift the bent fork
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation that his father was literally a forklift
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist robot family backstory. Works on multiple levels - robot family tree and explains his inability.

jokes.text: Wow. Bender is really committed to his new Jethrovian identity.
jokes.setup: Observing Billy/Bender's convincing performance
jokes.punchline: Made-up demonym 'Jethrovian' for hillbilly culture
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay creating a fake demonym from 'Jethro'. Good academic-sounding term for hillbilly culture.

jokes.text: Examine? Heck, if you can find it, you can keep it.
jokes.setup: Professor wanting to examine Billy's brain
jokes.punchline: Billy joking about not having a brain to find
jokes.explanation: Good hillbilly self-deprecation. Classic 'dumb country folk' humor delivered with good economy.

jokes.text: You sure you don't want to kill all humans?
jokes.setup: Fry's final attempt to trigger Bender's old personality
jokes.punchline: Reference to Bender's classic catchphrase and murderous tendencies
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bender's famous murderous tendencies. Fry's desperate final test of the old personality.

jokes.text: I love all humans.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he wants to kill all humans
jokes.punchline: Complete personality reversal - loving instead of wanting to kill humans
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal callback - complete opposite of Bender's signature misanthropy. Shows total personality change.

jokes.text: Francis is a way better friend than Bender ever was.
jokes.setup: Fry comparing Francis to Bender
jokes.punchline: Ironic praise of the person sent to kill Bender as a better friend
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows Francis is there to kill Bender while Fry praises him as a friend.

jokes.text: God bless Grandma and Grandpa... They're next!
jokes.setup: Billy saying prayers when threatened
jokes.punchline: Threatening his own grandparents after blessing them
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor subversion - blessing turning into threat. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: John (bleep) Zoidberg!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg correcting that his name isn't Slick
jokes.punchline: Dramatic full name declaration with middle initial obscenity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's rare moment of badassery with dramatic name declaration.

jokes.text: That offer was for a limited time only.
jokes.setup: Clamps saying 'all-you-can-kill lobster fest' while being defeated
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's matter-of-fact correction like it's a restaurant promotion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery treating death threat like expired restaurant deal. Perfect Zoidberg character moment.

jokes.text: Because our friend Francis here is in reality a bad murderer.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining why he fought Francis
jokes.punchline: Understated description of assassin as merely a 'bad murderer'
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor - calling professional assassin just a 'bad murderer'. Typical Zoidberg innocent perspective.

jokes.text: Bender, you two-timing jerk! I put off my singing career for you!
jokes.setup: Bella discovering Bender's deception
jokes.punchline: Claiming she sacrificed a singing career for their relationship
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship drama humor - claiming sacrificed career for failed relationship. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Bender was a hell of a squealer. Maybe the best there ever was.
jokes.setup: Donbot giving eulogy for Bender
jokes.punchline: Praising him for being a good informant, which is an insult in mob context
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - praising someone for the very thing that got them killed. Perfect mob boss perspective.

jokes.text: Bender? I'm-a no Bender. You got-a me-a mixed uppa with another-a robot.
jokes.setup: Pizza delivery revealing Bender is alive
jokes.punchline: Terrible Italian accent disguise that fools no one
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender disguise humor - obviously fake accent and denial. Consistent with his 'owner of disguise' character.

jokes.text: So that moon hillbilly who got murdered was just an innocent husband and father.
jokes.setup: Realizing Bender is alive and someone else died
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of innocent victim's death
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor acknowledging collateral damage. Leela's matter-of-fact delivery adds to the dark comedy.

jokes.text: You do, and I'll (bleep) gut you like a fish.
jokes.setup: Someone about to cut the pizza
jokes.punchline: Bender's violent overreaction to pizza cutting
jokes.explanation: Good return to normal Bender - violent overreaction to mundane situation. Shows he's back to his old self.
